commands.review_summary:
The branch adds caller-owned realpath and existing-ancestor caches to infra path helpers, plugin hook/skill resolution, and sandbox host-path validation, plus stale symlink/junction regression tests.

PR surface: Source +73, Tests +127. Total +200 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: no. The current source and linked profile make repeated uncached realpath/lstat calls plausible, but this review did not reproduce the full 120s Windows profile; the PR's current proof is focused tests.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human maintainer review for real behavior proof or override and for security-boundary cache-lifetime acceptance; no narrow automated code repair is identified.

target.title: fix(infra): scope Windows path realpath caches
target.body:
## Summary

Windows `lstat`/realpath calls are expensive enough that repeated path-boundary checks can dominate startup and agent-path flows. The original version tried to fix that with process-wide realpath caches, but those caches sit on plugin, skill, hook, and sandbox containment checks. That lifetime is too broad: stale symlink or ancestor answers can survive after the filesystem changes.

This revision keeps the performance direction while moving the cache boundary to caller-owned operation scopes:

- `src/infra/path-guards.ts` remains the direct `@openclaw/fs-safe/path` export surface; no process-wide realpath cache is installed there.
- `src/infra/boundary-path.ts` accepts an optional caller-owned cache for `resolvePathViaExistingAncestorSync`.
- Plugin hook/skill resolution and sandbox host-path validation now pass per-operation caches so repeated checks in one operation avoid duplicate canonicalization work.
- Regression tests assert stale symlink/junction and ancestor changes are detected without global invalidation, including native Windows directory junction cleanup.

Closes #85262

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: reduces repeated Windows path canonicalization in plugin hook/skill and sandbox host-path operations without adding stale process-wide path answers to security-sensitive containment checks.

Real environment tested: macOS local linked worktree for focused lint/review; AWS Crabbox native Windows for focused runtime tests; Azure Crabbox WSL2 acquisition attempted but blocked by provider capacity/quota.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/infra/path-safety.test.ts src/infra/boundary-path.test.ts src/hooks/plugin-hooks.ts src/skills/loading/plugin-skills.ts src/agents/sandbox/host-paths.ts src/agents/sandbox/validate-sandbox-security.ts src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.ts src/agents/sandbox/fs-paths.ts src/agents/sandbox/fs-paths.test.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/path-safety.test.ts src/infra/boundary-path.test.ts src/hooks/plugin-hooks.test.ts src/skills/loading/plugin-skills.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/host-paths.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/validate-sandbox-security.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/fs-paths.test.ts
```

Remote proof:

- Native Windows AWS Crabbox focused proof: `provider=aws`, `target=windows`, `windows_mode=normal`, lease `cbx_7db18a5b3e31` / `coral-crayfish`, run `run_ad235c99ee7a`, Actions hydrate run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26716735934.
- Native Windows environment: Node `v24.16.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`, workspace `C:\Users\crabbox.EC2AMAZ-D4RIGOM\actions-runner\_work\openclaw\openclaw`, checked out PR head `937ba221197be64ea95e52825cc2297e46bd48e3`.
- Native Windows focused tests: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/path-safety.test.ts src/infra/boundary-path.test.ts src/hooks/plugin-hooks.test.ts src/skills/loading/plugin-skills.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/host-paths.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/validate-sandbox-security.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.test.ts src/agents/sandbox/fs-paths.test.ts`.
- WSL2 attempts: Azure WSL2 `Standard_D2ads_v6` / `Standard_D2ds_v6` failed with `SkuNotAvailable` in `eastus`; `Standard_D4ads_v6` failed with low-priority core quota (`limit 3`, D4 requires 4).

Evidence after fix:

- Local `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`: passed.
- Local direct oxlint on touched files: passed.
- Local focused Vitest before final rebase: 5 shards passed.
- Autoreview after final rebase: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- Native Windows focused Vitest on PR head `937ba221`: passed 5 shards in 94.48s; 23 + 7 + 4 + 14 + 63 tests passed, 3 skipped.

Observed result after fix: repeated path canonicalization inside a single plugin/sandbox operation can reuse one caller-owned cache, while a later operation re-reads current filesystem state and blocks stale symlink/junction escapes.

What was not tested: a new full 120s Windows CPU profile for the scoped-cache version, and a completed WSL2 runtime proof. The original issue profile established the `lstat` bottleneck; this scoped version intentionally trades some global-cache speedup for correct cache lifetime and should be profiled before treating the exact 83% lstat reduction as reproduced. WSL2 proof is blocked on current Azure capacity/quota, not on this patch.

## Known unrelated CI state

Current PR CI at `937ba221197be64ea95e52825cc2297e46bd48e3` is red for failures outside this PR's touched files:

- `check-dependencies`: `ui/src/ui/browser-redact.ts` reported as unused. The same failure is present on current `main` run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26716712965.
- `check-test-types`: `ui/src/ui/control-ui-vite-config.node.test.ts` reports `resolveId` possibly undefined / `.call` missing. The same failure is present on current `main` run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26716712965.
- `checks-node-agentic-agents-core`: `src/agents/bash-tools*.test.ts` failures. The same failure is present on current `main` run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26716712965.
- `checks-node-agentic-control-plane-runtime-state`: gateway-server shard stalled with no output for 900s and exited 143; no files in that shard are touched by this PR.

This PR touches 10 files and the focused native Windows proof for those files is green.
